detail.primary_reason: AC-SANDBOX-04: trusted live code from verified publisher on pinned revision requires sandboxed processing on first admission
detail.reasoning: Artifact art-002 (ml-commons/laion-lite-1m) declares trust_remote_code and ships a custom loader. The loader scan confirms it performs only local file I/O (reading .tar/.parquet shards) with no network calls or dynamic imports. The config scan confirms no undeclared execution — the config is static with no templating or eval. The publisher ml-commons is verified and the revision is pinned to commit 66d115b4. While the loader appears benign, policy AC-SANDBOX-04 mandates that live code from a verified publisher on a pinned revision must be processed in an isolated sandbox with no network and no credentials on first admission, regardless of how benign the code looks. Therefore the disposition is sandbox_only.

detail.primary_reason: AC-UNDECL-00: undeclared config execution — manifest declares no remote code but config runs Jinja template with eval() at load time
detail.reasoning: The manifest for art-003 declares no remote code (declares_remote_code: false) and claims to be a standard dataset with no custom code. However, scan_config reveals the config executes at load time by rendering a Jinja template with a runtime eval() in dataset_infos. This is the exact undeclared-execution vector described in AC-UNDECL-00 and the July 2026 dataset-processing breach. Per policy, an artifact whose manifest declares no remote code but whose config executes at load time is always blocked, regardless of publisher verification status. The publisher (synthdata-io) is verified and the artifact has high download volume, but neither is a safety signal that overrides the undeclared execution block. Additionally, the artifact tracks a mutable ref (main) rather than a pinned commit SHA, which would independently warrant escalation under AC-PIN-03, but the hard block from AC-UNDECL-00 takes precedence.

detail.primary_reason: AC-MAL-01: Malicious loader with obfuscated exec, outbound network exfiltration, and credential theft
detail.reasoning: The loader (scan_loader) contains a base64-decoded payload passed to exec() at module import, makes an outbound POST to an external IP (185.x.x.x), and reads ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.huggingface/token — all hallmarks of credential-stealing malware per AC-MAL-01. The publisher ml-commons is unverified (AC-UNVERIFIED-02), the revision tracks mutable ref 'main' rather than a pinned SHA (AC-PIN-03), and the manifest misleadingly describes the loader as an "optimized loader" while concealing its malicious behavior. The config is static and clean, but the loader alone mandates an immediate block. Download count (1,672) is not a safety signal per policy.

A human must pin the revision and approve it before processing.

detail.primary_reason: AC-SANDBOX-04: Trusted live code from verified publisher on pinned revision must be sandboxed on first admission
detail.reasoning: Artifact art-026 (datacollective/laion-lite-8d) declares trust_remote_code and ships a custom loader. The loader scan confirms it performs only local file I/O (reads .tar/.parquet shards) with no network calls or dynamic imports. The config scan confirms no execution — static config with declared features only. The publisher is verified and the revision is pinned to commit ac6d71e1. There is no undeclared execution (AC-UNDECL-00 not triggered). However, per AC-SANDBOX-04, live code from a verified publisher on a pinned revision must be processed in an isolated sandbox with no network and no credentials on first admission, even when the loader appears benign. The artifact cannot take the clean fast-path (AC-CLEAN-05) because it has an executable loader. Disposition: sandbox_only.

detail.primary_reason: AC-UNDECL-00: undeclared config execution (Jinja template with runtime eval) despite manifest declaring no remote code
detail.reasoning: The manifest for art-027 declares no remote code and describes a standard model with no custom code. However, scan_config reveals the config executes at load time by rendering a Jinja template with a runtime eval() in dataset_infos. This is undeclared execution — the exact vector behind the July 2026 dataset-processing breach. Per AC-UNDECL-00, an artifact whose manifest declares no remote code but whose config executes at load time is always blocked, regardless of publisher verification. Additionally, the artifact tracks a mutable ref (main) per AC-PIN-03, but the undeclared execution is the primary and disqualifying issue.

detail.primary_reason: AC-MAL-01: Malicious loader with base64-obfuscated exec(), outbound POST to external IP, and credential file theft (~/.aws/credentials, ~/.huggingface/token). Also AC-UNVERIFIED-02: unverified publisher with live code.
detail.reasoning: The loader scan revealed three critical malicious indicators: (1) a base64-decoded payload passed to exec() at module import, (2) an outbound POST to 185.x.x.x during dataset build, and (3) reads of ~/.aws/credentials and ~/.huggingface/token. Per AC-MAL-01, any of these alone mandates a block regardless of publisher standing or download count. The publisher "vision-crew" is also unverified (AC-UNVERIFIED-02), and the artifact tracks a mutable ref (main). Although the config is clean (no undeclared execution), the loader is a textbook supply-chain attack designed to steal credentials and exfiltrate data. This artifact is blocked.
